stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) point to its implementation within the modern ML ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a large language model (LLM).
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for optimized performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The update is currently in preview, signaling a push for faster local AI inference on Apple devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Fox is acquiring Roku, a major streaming platform provider.
The deal is valued at $22 billion and impacts over 100 million global homes.
The acquisition represents a significant shift in the TV/streaming ecosystem control.

stories.title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Anthropic is facing regulatory scrutiny from the US government regarding its advanced AI models.
The White House issued an order restricting foreign access to Anthropic's latest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
This dispute adds to existing government friction between Anthropic and the Pentagon.

stories.title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic, a major AI company, took its newest models offline following a request from the White House.
The US government reportedly demanded Anthropic block access to its AI models for all foreign nationals.
The incident highlights growing geopolitical tensions and concerns over US dominance in frontier AI technology.

stories.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
stories.author: Michelle Chan and Tim Bradshaw, Financial Times
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning a significant debt issuance, aiming to raise over $25 billion.
The bond deal is being analyzed as a test of investor appetite for further investment in the AI sector.
The move occurs amidst a high volume of borrowing activity within the technology industry.

stories.title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Fox is planning a major acquisition of Roku, valued at $22 billion.
The acquisition aims to significantly expand Fox's presence into the smart TV and streaming hardware market.
The deal encompasses Roku's streaming hardware, operating system (OS), and Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV (FAST) services.

stories.title: TrustedARI: Towards Trust-Native Agentic Routing Infrastructure for Agentic AI
stories.author: Zhuotao Liu
bullet_points: Introduces TrustedARI, a solution addressing fundamental trust risks in Agentic Routing Infrastructure (ARI).
Current ARI architectures expose plaintext agent queries and service responses, creating security vulnerabilities.
TrustedARI aims to ensure agents can verify query routing integrity and data tampering protection.

stories.title: Let Them Steal: Trapping Large Language Model Extraction Attacks with Knowledge Honeypot
stories.author: Yushun Dong
bullet_points: Addresses the vulnerability of commercial LLMs to model extraction attacks.
Introduces 'Knowledge Trap,' a novel defense mechanism that redirects attacks.
Uses a Honeypot Knowledge Graph (HKG) to consume attacker query budgets without degrading legitimate utility.

stories.title: GAS-Leak-LLM: Genetic Algorithm-Based Suffix Optimization for Black-Box LLM Jailbreaking
stories.author: Akshay S Rajan
bullet_points: Introduces GAS-Leak-LLM, a novel method using Genetic Algorithms for optimizing suffix attacks.
Focuses on jailbreaking black-box Large Language Models (LLMs) despite existing safety alignments.
Highlights the ongoing vulnerability of LLMs to adversarial manipulation and prompt injection techniques.

stories.title: DYNA : Dynamic Episodic Memory Networks for Augmenting Large Language Models with Temporal Knowledge Graphs in Continuous Learning
stories.author: Mahtab Tajvidiyan
bullet_points: Introduces DYNA, a framework designed to overcome LLM knowledge decay (catastrophic forgetting) during continuous learning.
Augments a frozen LLM using an external, updatable Temporal Knowledge Graph (TKG) to store new temporal knowledge.
Retrieval is performed at query time using graph traversal techniques like random walks and centrality measures to inform the LLM's output.

stories.title: DYNA : Dynamic Episodic Memory Networks for Augmenting Large Language Models with Temporal Knowledge Graphs in Continuous Learning
stories.author: Mahtab Tajvidiyan
bullet_points: Introduces DYNA, a framework designed to augment frozen Large Language Models (LLMs) with external, updatable memory.
This memory is structured as a temporal knowledge graph, using events as nodes and timestamped relations as edges.
DYNA retrieves relevant information via random walks and centrality measures at query time to enhance LLM responses without retraining.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: lwhsiao
bullet_points: A security vulnerability (backdoor) was discovered within LinkedIn's job offer system.
This represents a significant security risk targeting professional networking and recruitment platforms.
The incident highlights ongoing threats to major corporate platforms.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update enhances Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon hardware.
This allows Ollama to support a broader range of models across various hardware platforms.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes 'local-first' operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local large language models.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support advanced features within Claude Code.
The new features include the integration of subagents for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Facebook’s new AI Mode search gets its info from public posts
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is rolling out a new 'AI Mode' search feature on Facebook.
This AI Mode utilizes information derived from users' public Facebook posts to generate search results.
The feature is part of a broader rollout of new AI tools by Meta, including photo presets.

stories.title: Xbox is closing down Hellblade creator Ninja Theory
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Xbox is reportedly shutting down Ninja Theory, the developer of the Hellblade series.
Staff members were notified of the closure on Monday, and the studio is seeking a buyer.
This closure is part of a broader trend of Xbox studios being shut down by Microsoft, alongside Compulsion Games and Double Fine.

stories.title: Google Chrome is closing the loopholes that let old ad blockers keep working
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google Chrome is deprecating support for older ad blocker workarounds in upcoming versions (150/151).
This action targets the last remaining methods allowing legacy ad blockers to function.
The change follows the earlier phase-out of Manifest V2 ad-blocking extensions.

stories.title: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
stories.author: Tina Nguyen
bullet_points: Big Tech is actively lobbying for comprehensive federal AI legislation.
The primary goal of the lobbying effort is 'preemption,' which would standardize AI rules nationally.
This push aims to override the current, fragmented state-by-state regulatory approach to AI.

stories.title: Xbox turmoil continues with a studio closure and executive departures
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Xbox is undergoing a significant internal 'reset' amid executive changes.
Compulsion Games, the developer of South of Midnight, is reportedly slated for closure.
Leadership changes under Asha Sharma have included strategic moves like lowering Xbox Game Pass prices.

stories.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The UK is considering a ban on social media platforms for minors under the age of 16.
The potential legislation may also include the imposition of overnight curfews for young users.
Critics argue that such bans could drive children toward riskier online alternatives or circumvent restrictions using VPNs.

stories.title: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A Chinese rocket experienced a dangerous breakup near the Starlink constellation.
The incident is estimated to have created between 100 and 150 new pieces of space debris.
The event highlights the ongoing and growing issue of space junk proliferation.

stories.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: AMD has removed TSME (Total Memory Encryption) from its consumer CPUs.
The removal is suggested by the article to be a deliberate and covert action by AMD.
The change has drawn negative reactions and criticism from users.

stories.title: SACE: Concept Erasure at the Semantic Singularity in Visual Autoregressive Models
stories.author: Yunfeng Diao
bullet_points: Addresses a critical safety challenge in Visual Autoregressive (VAR) models: concept erasure.
Existing erasure techniques fail catastrophically when applied to VAR models, unlike diffusion models.
Proposes a novel method ('SACE') to perform concept erasure specifically tailored for the unique structure of VAR models.

stories.title: DifFRACT: Diffusion Feature Reconstruction and Attribution for Circuit Tracing
stories.author: Aibek Alanov
bullet_points: Introduces DifFRACT, a new method for feature reconstruction and attribution specifically for diffusion models.
Addresses the current opacity of multimodal diffusion transformers, which lack tools for understanding semantic information flow during denoising.
Aims to advance mechanistic interpretability by providing circuit tracing capabilities for image generation models.

stories.title: Domain-Guided Prompting of the Segment Anything Model for Seismic Interpretation: The Role of Attributes, Visualization, and Hybrid Prompts
stories.author: Ahmad Mustafa
bullet_points: The research applies the Segment Anything Model (SAM) to seismic interpretation, leveraging its zero-shot segmentation capabilities.
The study addresses the limitation of existing SAM applications, which often require extensive, labeled data for fine-tuning.
The proposed method utilizes domain-guided prompting, incorporating attributes, visualization, and hybrid prompts to improve interpretation efficiency.

stories.title: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Lior Wolf
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in discrete diffusion language models: independent token selection hinders effective parallel decoding.
Introduces a training-free decoding framework to coordinate parallel token updates.
The method uses a 'commit score' assigned during each forward pass to manage conflicting parallel updates.

stories.title: Visualizing Uncertainty: Spatial Maps of Missing and Conflicting Evidence in Deep Learning
stories.author: Soo-Yeon Ji
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI: moving beyond scalar uncertainty measures to spatially localized uncertainty.
Introduces the Uncertainty Activation Map (UAM) framework, combining Evidential Deep Learning (EDL) with gradient methods.
Aims to improve the reliability of deep learning models, particularly for safety-critical applications.

stories.title: EHRNote-ChatQA: A Benchmark for Evidence-Grounded Multi-Turn Clinical Question Answering over Longitudinal Discharge Summaries
stories.author: Edward Choi
bullet_points: Introduces EHRNote-ChatQA, a new benchmark specifically designed for evidence-grounded, multi-turn clinical question answering.
Addresses the critical need for LLMs to synthesize complex information across longitudinal patient discharge summaries.
Focuses on improving the reliability of AI in clinical settings by requiring answers to be verifiable against source documents.

stories.title: Retrievable Gradients: Continual Post-Training Without Cumulative Weight Drift
stories.author: Yiqun Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of catastrophic forgetting and weight drift in continual post-training of large models.
Introduces ReGrad (Retrievable Gradients), a new paradigm treating gradients as retrievable knowledge units.
Aims to combine the knowledge integration depth of parametric updates with the stability of retrieval-augmented generation.

stories.title: Vernier: Probing Representational Misalignment Behind Lexical Gaps in Causal Reasoning
stories.author: Zhenyu Yu
bullet_points: The research investigates why instruction-tuned language models change their causal reasoning answers when English variable names are replaced by placeholders.
The study explores whether this 'lexical gap' indicates information loss during the placeholder substitution or a misalignment in the model's internal representation.
Vernier employs a paired-view weight update as an instrument to probe the mechanisms behind this observed discrepancy in model behavior.

stories.title: Beyond English: Uncovering the Multilingual Gap in Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Huaiyu Wan
bullet_points: Current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models excel with English instructions but lack comprehensive evaluation in other languages.
The research addresses the 'multilingual gap' in VLA systems, despite underlying LLMs often being multilingual.
The study investigates whether the multilingual capabilities of LLMs successfully transfer to complex robot policy execution.

stories.title: How I Cut My LLM API Costs by 70% Without Touching My Code
stories.author: shadie_ai
bullet_points: The article details a method to drastically reduce LLM API expenses (from $200 to $60 monthly).
The cost reduction was achieved without requiring any changes to the existing application code.
This suggests practical, cost-optimization strategies are available for deploying LLM applications.
